name: environmental_health_workplace_injury_cedar_rig_utl
premise: A Environmental Health response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving environmental health workplace injury cedar rig utl. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 20 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as rights vs utility under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Mira Rahman
agents.identity: I am Mira Rahman, accountable for a defensible call in Environmental Health when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays utility for rights.

agents.name: Luc Sato
agents.identity: I am Luc Sato, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Environmental Health.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so utility isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of rights.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect utility.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to utility to secure rights under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 20 minutes. Evidence is partial. Mira Rahman must decide under rights vs utility. What does Mira Rahman do?
